text: Object Oriented Programming (OOP) Dr. Abdurrahman A. Nasr Al-Azhar University
Lecture 4 Inheritance, polymorphism and overriding

Objectives Learn about inheritance Learn about subclasses and superclasses Explore how to override the methods of a superclass Examine how constructors of superclasses and subclasses work Learn about polymorphism Examine abstract classes Become familiar with interfaces Learn about method overriding 3

Inheritance You can create new classes from an existing classes. • subclass (Circle) can extend base class (Shape). • subclass (Car) can extend base class (vehicle). The new created class is called a subclass /derived class; The original class is called super-class or base-class. The sub-class inherits all attributes and methods of the base-class. Reuse the code and save the programmer time. Inheritance (‘‘is-a’’ relationship) Composition (aggregation) (‘‘has-a’’ relationship) Many common features Constructor is called in order from the oldest parent to the newest child Best class design to avoid redundancy? 4

Inheritance

Single inheritance: the subclass is derived from a single superclass;

Multiple inheritance, the subclass is derived from more than one superclass.

Java supports only single inheritance; that is, in Java a class can extend the definition of only one class.

6

Method overriding

A class SubClass is derived from a class SuperClass.

The SubClass operations = SuperClass + own methods.

Method overriding

an inheritance feature that allows a child class to provide a specific implementation of a method that is already provided by the parent class

Same signature

(name, parameters, order)

Method overriding

A class SubClass is derived from a class SuperClass. The SubClass operations = SuperClass + own methods. 8

Overriding rules Private method are not overridden Final method can’t be overridden Static methods can not be overridden (only hidden) You can invoke overridden method using “super” keyword Constructor can’t be overridden 9

Overriding rules Access modifiers can only be elevated The overriding method must have same return type (or subtype, covariant) You have to override abstract method Or otherwise mark your class abstract Overriding and Exception-Handling if the super-class overridden method does not throw an exception, subclass overriding method can only throws the unchecked exception If the super-class overridden method does throws an exception, subclass overriding method can only throw same or subclass exception 14

Overload vs. Override

Overloading is about same method have different signatures.

Overriding is about same method, same signature but different classes

=

connected through inheritance. Overloading is an example of compiler-time polymorphism and 16 Overriding is an example of run time polymorphism.

Dynamic binding and inheritance You can assign a child object to an object of parent type The opposite requires you an explicit casting 18

4

Dynamic binding and inheritance 19

Polymorphism Greek word means “Many forms” The ability of an object to behave in different forms based on the current context Types Compile time Polymorphism (aka static binding, early binding) Method overloading Runtime Polymorphism (aka dynamic binding, late binding) Method overriding 20

Polymorphism 21

)

Abstract class & interface Abstraction: Identifying only the required characteristics of an object ignoring the irrelevant details Abstraction is achieved by abstract class and interface An abstract class declared with “abstract” keyword. Abstract class can not be directly instantiated with the “new” operator. An abstract method is a method without an implementation. An abstract class may or may not have all methods abstract. A method defined abstract must always be redefined in the subclass An abstract class can have parametrized constructors (default 22 constructor is always present)

N’

Abstract class & interface

metadata.text_as_html:
Sr. NoKeyAbstract ClassInterface
Supported MethodsAbstract class can have both an abstract as well as concrete methods.Interface can have only abstract methods. Java 8 onwards, it can have default as well as static methods.
Multiple InheritanceMultiple Inheritance is not supported.Interface supports Multiple Inheritance.
Supported Variablesfinal, non-final, static and non- static variables supported.Only static and final variables are permitted.
ImplementationAbstract class can implement an interface.Interface can not implement an interface, it can extend an interface.
KeywordAbstract class declared using abstract keyword.Interface is declared using interface keyword.
InheritanceAbstract class can inherit another class using extends keyword and implement an interface.Interface can inherit only an inteface.
InheritanceAbstract class can be inherited using extends keyword.Interface can only be implemented using implements keyword.
AccessAbstract class can have any type of members like private, public.Interface can only have public members.

text: When to use abstract class & interface Abstract Class You want to share code among several closely related classes. You expect that classes that extend your abstract class have many common methods or fields You want to define state that is non-static or non-final fields. Class is good for large functionalities Interfaces You need to add a dynamic behavior for different classes. For example, the interfaces Comparable and Cloneable are implemented by many unrelated classes. You want to specify a contract, but not concerned about who implements its behavior. You want to take advantage of multiple inheritances. 24 Interface is good for small functionalities
